plan: 1. Create the PlayGuide page component at src/features/guide/PlayGuide.tsx — Convert the play_guide.md content into a structured React component with semantic HTML sections: 'Structure at a glance', 'Three ways to play' (with 3 subsections), 'Rituals', 'Pairing references', 'Flavour text'. Use the existing Layout wrapper pattern and design tokens from tokens.css.

2. Create the guide page CSS module at src/features/guide/PlayGuide.module.css — Style the guide for readable typography on mobile and desktop using existing design tokens (--font-family for headings, --font-family-sans for body, spacing scale, color tokens). Ensure responsive layout with appropriate max-width for readability (~65ch), proper heading hierarchy, and tier color indicators (green/amber/blue with text labels per accessibility requirements).
3. Export the PlayGuide component from src/features/guide/index.ts — Replace the empty stub export with the PlayGuide component export.

4. Add client-side routing to App.tsx — Since no router exists yet and React Router is not installed, implement a lightweight hash-based or path-based router using window.location and React state (no new dependency needed for this simple case). Route '/guide' to PlayGuide, keep '/' as the existing home content. Alternatively, install react-router-dom if the team prefers — but a minimal custom approach avoids adding a dependency for 4 static routes.

5. Update Nav.tsx to support client-side navigation — Convert <a> tags to use onClick handlers that update route state (or React Router Link components if react-router-dom is chosen), preventing full page reloads.

6. Write unit tests in tests/unit/play-guide.test.tsx — Test that the PlayGuide component renders all required sections (structure, ways to play, rituals, etiquette), test that headings use proper semantic hierarchy, test that the navigation link to /guide exists.
7. Add an E2E smoke test in tests/e2e/ — Verify the /guide route loads, key sections are visible, and navigation from home to guide works.
8. Run validation suite: npm run lint, npm run typecheck, npm run test, and npm run test:e2e to confirm all checks pass.
testing_recommendations: Unit test: PlayGuide component renders all 5 major sections (Structure at a glance, Three ways to play, Rituals, Pairing references, Flavour text)
Unit test: All section headings use correct semantic heading levels (h2 for sections, h3 for subsections)
Unit test: Tier indicators display both color and text labels (accessibility requirement)
Unit test: Navigation component includes a link to /guide
E2E test: Navigating to /guide renders the play guide page with visible content
E2E test: Clicking the Guide nav link from any page navigates to the guide
Manual test: Verify readable typography at mobile (< 768px) and desktop breakpoints
Run full validation: npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test && npm run test:e2e
risks: No router exists yet — this ticket requires introducing client-side routing, which affects App.tsx and Nav.tsx (shared infrastructure). A minimal custom router avoids a new dependency but may need to be replaced later; installing react-router-dom is more standard but adds a dependency.
Updating Nav.tsx from plain <a> tags to client-side navigation could break existing links or tests (tests/unit/layout-shell.test.tsx tests the nav).

The play guide content is static markdown text hardcoded into JSX — future content changes require a code change. Could mitigate by loading the markdown file at build time, but that adds complexity beyond the ticket scope.
Other feature stubs (browse, play) also need routing — the routing approach chosen here sets a precedent. Keep it extensible.
